role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; co-subject of a joint oral history with Carolyn Miller (catalog entry 12). The slug suffix distinguishes him from other public figures of the same name.
ai_reading: James Miller (CRHP) shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 12 (catalog slug 'carolyn-miller-and-james-miller'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: James Miller's Civil Rights History Project oral history is cross-referenced against the Library of Congress published transcript. James Miller (CRHP) is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 12 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for James Miller (CRHP); the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 12
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.alt: Claiborne County Courthouse, Port Gibson, Mississippi
gallery.caption: The Claiborne County Courthouse in Port Gibson, Mississippi, the county seat where James Miller grew up, joined the NAACP youth group, and took part in the Port Gibson boycotts described in his oral history.
